mnemonics: M.I.T.M. stands for Message Interception & Tampering Menace.
Picture a sneaky messenger secretly reading and changing your private mail between you and a friend.
keyPoints: An attacker secretly relays and potentially alters the communication between two parties who believe they are directly communicating.
It is a confidentiality AND integrity attack, as the attacker can both eavesdrop and modify data.
Common techniques include ARP Spoofing on local networks and DNS Spoofing to redirect traffic to malicious sites.
commonMistakes: Assuming HTTPS alone is always sufficient protection; improperly configured sites or stolen certificates can still be vulnerable.
Believing MitM attacks only happen on public Wi-Fi; they can occur on any network, including corporate and home networks.
